TextENGNew: (...How many years has it been since I gazed up at the sky with such a light heart?)
I filled my lungs with a deep breath as I looked up at the brilliant blue sky.
...The warm air carried a sweet fragrance, brimming with refreshing clarity—it felt wonderful.
A glorious spring day.
It was as though the weather itself was celebrating my new beginning... though perhaps that's a touch grandiose.
``...@k@vS20/36/VTK2_natsumi2003. All right... I can do this...``
Stepping out the front door, I walked down the road toward school... pressing a hand to my chest to check my heartbeat.
My pulse was steady, with no trace of palpitations, chills, or cold sweat.
...My heart was perfectly calm.
Every time I used to step outside, I was plagued by anxiety and dizziness... but now there was no heaviness, no suffocation. My shoulders, my legs, my entire body felt as light as down feathers.
``...Huh?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_StuFa2002. Could that girl be...?``
``Yeah... that's right.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_StuFb2002. But there's no way...``
I passed several students in uniform along the way... and shortly after, I felt the prickling sting of someone's gaze and heard the soft murmur of whispered voices.
...Not long ago, that kind of 'noise' would have caused me unbearable anguish.
Whenever I felt eyes on me, my heart would pound harder with every step, and chills would race through my body.
The moment I thought they were gossiping about me, great beads of sweat would erupt from my face and back, I'd grow dizzy, and then... I'd faint.
That's why I couldn't walk outside alone.
I hated talking to people. I hated even seeing their faces.
But... now, it didn't bother me at all.
It was as though I were walking in a dream... as if I were strolling through someplace unreal, and I could simply ignore anything I disliked without even trying.
Unpleasant images, sounds, and other sensory input.
...All that bothersome 'garbage' faded into a dim haze, and my world was composed only of what suited me.
(...That's right, don't think about it.@k@vS20/36/VTK2_natsumi2005. As long as I don't overthink things, I'll be fine.)
The people around me weren't deliberately staring at me.
And even if they did look, as long as I paid them no mind, they were merely part of the scenery.
Yes—like stones by the roadside.
Like weeds growing quietly in the shade.
If they don't speak to me, it's no different from them not being there at all.
That's all I need to tell myself.
And thanks to the medication the doctor gave me, I was able to shift my thinking in exactly that way.
``Ahaha... ahahahahaha...``
That's right.
That incident—the one that made me shut myself in—if I had simply refused to engage with it from the start, none of it would have happened.
changeReason: Old translation says 'dealt with everything like I am now' (applying current coping); new says 'refused to engage with it from the start,' which more accurately reflects the JPN (not engaging at all vs applying current strategy).

TextENGNew: Even when they came at me waving scissors, if I had just ignored them, they would have lost interest and backed off.
Yes... as long as you don't get involved with anyone, nothing will happen.
There's nothing to fret about.
And surely the other kids feel the same way.
Even the one who taunted me with the scissors.
Even the one who got hurt.
The classmates who watched in silence, the teacher—all of them... would surely prefer, for their own reasons, to pretend it never happened.
So... I'll pretend it 'never happened' too.
That's fine.
That should be perfectly fine.
I'm sure of it...
